title: Roasted Thai Split Peas Boat Noodles
description: A crispy and flavorful Thai dish featuring roasted split peas, served in a cedar plank boat noodle form, perfect for a weeknight dinner.

ingredients.name: split peas
ingredients.notes: dried green or yellow split peas

instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, toss the split peas with coconut oil, Thai red curry paste, fish sauce, lime juice, brown sugar, salt, and pepper until they are evenly coated.
Spread the split peas on a baking sheet and roast for 20 minutes, or until crispy and lightly browned.
While the split peas are roasting, prepare the cedar planks by cutting them into boat noodle shapes.
Once the split peas are done, remove them from the oven and let them cool slightly.
To assemble the dish, place a portion of the roasted split peas onto each cedar plank boat noodle.
Garnish with chopped fresh cilantro and serve immediately.
Serve with your favorite Thai-inspired toppings, such as diced mango or sliced chili peppers.
Be careful when handling the hot cedar planks.
Repeat the process until all the ingredients are used up.
